hook.scenario: When you look at 'average package' stats for an engineering college, a few 40 LPA outliers drag up the 3.5 LPA mass.
hook.question: If averages hide the real distribution, how do we manipulate the total sum to figure out the true numbers?

mental_model.intuition: Average is the 'equal distribution' point. If everyone threw their money in a pot and split it evenly, that's the average.
mental_model.description: The sum of all observations divided by the number of observations. It acts as the center of gravity for the dataset.

pattern_discovery.setup: Observe what happens to the average when every single item in the dataset changes uniformly.
pattern_discovery.prompt: If you perform a basic arithmetic operation on every element, what happens to the average?
pattern_discovery.rule: The exact same operation applies directly to the average. The entire 'center of gravity' shifts.

formula.statement: Average = Sum of all values / Total number of values.
formula.explain: Sum = Average × Number of values. Most tricky average problems are solved by thinking in terms of the Total Sum.

shortcuts.title: Assumed Mean (Deviation) Method
shortcuts.trick: Guess an average. Find how much each number deviates from your guess. Average the deviations and add back to your guess.
shortcuts.example: Average of 102, 105, 98. Guess 100. Deviations: +2, +5, -2. Sum of dev = +5. Avg dev = +5/3. True avg = 101.66.

shortcuts.title: Inclusion / Exclusion
shortcuts.trick: If a new item joins and increases the average by x, its value is: (Old Average) + (New Total Size × x).
shortcuts.example: 10 items avg 20. New item makes avg 21. New item = 20 + (11 × 1) = 31.

common_mistakes.title: Average Speed Trap
common_mistakes.explanation: Average speed is NOT the arithmetic mean of speeds (e.g., 40kmph and 60kmph != 50kmph avg). It is Total Distance / Total Time.

common_mistakes.title: Ignoring Sample Size
common_mistakes.explanation: You cannot average two averages directly unless they represent the exact same number of items. Use the weighted average.

recall_questions.q: What is the average of the first 5 prime numbers?
If the average of 5 numbers is 10, what is their sum?
Average of 4, 8, 12, 16 is 10. If we add 5 to each, what is the new average?

oa_questions.question: The average age of a class of 30 students is 15 years. If the teacher's age is included, the average increases by 1. What is the teacher's age?
oa_questions.approach: Teacher brings enough to match the old average (15) PLUS enough to give everyone including herself +1 year (31 people × 1 = 31). Age = 15 + 31 = 46.

oa_questions.question: The average of 50 numbers is 38. If two numbers, namely 45 and 55, are discarded, what is the average of the remaining numbers?
oa_questions.approach: Total Sum = 50 × 38 = 1900. Discard sum = 100. New sum = 1800. New count = 48. New avg = 1800 / 48 = 37.5.
